title: Audio: DP reactivation — the four decisions at speed

cues.label: Reactivation
cues.headline: You know DP; rebuild the speed
cues.narration: This is a strong-but-stale pattern, so the goal is recall speed: write the right recurrence from memory in under two minutes.

cues.label: The four decisions
cues.headline: State, recurrence, order, base
cues.narration: Every DP is four decisions in the same order: name the state, write the recurrence, pick the fill order, set the base case.

cues.label: Precise state
cues.headline: The state sentence is exact
cues.narration: A vague state makes the recurrence ambiguous; sharpen it to 'ending exactly at i' and the transition is forced.

cues.label: Kadane
cues.headline: A running scalar, extend or restart
cues.narration: Maximum subarray is best-here equals the max of x alone or best-here plus x; restart wins exactly when the run went negative.

cues.label: Knapsack
cues.headline: Rolling array, sweep descending
cues.narration: The 2D table collapses to one row; sweeping capacity high to low keeps each item used at most once.

cues.label: Direction flip
cues.headline: Ascending allows reuse
cues.narration: Coin change is the same table swept ascending so a coin can repeat — one machine, opposite loop direction.

cues.label: LIS
cues.headline: dp-ends-at-i, or patience tails
cues.narration: Longest increasing subsequence is dp ending at i in n squared, or a patience tails array with binary search in n log n.

cues.label: Traps
cues.headline: Order, base, vague state, sentinel
cues.narration: The bugs are fill order, base cases, a vague state, and an unreachable sentinel — not the idea itself.
